questions.question: Which layer in a three-tier LAN design is the fast-transport backbone that connects distribution layers and runs all Layer 3 with no spanning tree?
questions.explanation: The core layer is the fast-transport backbone used in large LANs to connect distribution layers; it is all Layer 3, has no spanning tree, and its focus is speed.

questions.question: What does the access layer typically perform?
options: QoS marking, port security, DAI, DHCP snooping, and PoE
OSPF routing and spanning tree between distribution switches
questions.explanation: The access layer is where end hosts connect; it typically performs QoS marking, port security, DAI, and DHCP snooping, and its switchports may be PoE-enabled.

questions.question: In a spine-leaf data-center topology, which statement is true?
options: Every leaf connects to every spine, and spines do not connect to each other
Spines connect to each other and to every leaf
Leaves connect to each other but not to spines
Only one spine serves all leaves
questions.explanation: In spine-leaf, every leaf connects to every spine, but spine switches do not connect to each other.

questions.question: What does SOHO stand for?
questions.explanation: SOHO stands for Small Office / Home Office network.

questions.question: Which VPN type is used between an end user's laptop and a company network, typically using TLS?
questions.explanation: A remote-access VPN is a VPN from an end device (laptop/phone) into a company network and typically uses TLS (e.g., Cisco AnyConnect).

questions.question: Which topology connects many devices to a single central device?
questions.explanation: A star topology has many devices connecting to a single central device.

questions.question: What defines a full-mesh topology?
options: Every device is directly connected to every other device
Some devices are connected to every other device, but not all
Many devices connect to one central device
Each site has a single connection to one ISP
questions.explanation: In a full mesh, every device is directly connected to every other device.

questions.question: What is east-west traffic?
options: Server-to-server traffic within the same part of the network
Traffic between access and core/Internet directions
Traffic leaving the enterprise toward an ISP
Management-plane traffic to the core layer
questions.explanation: East-west traffic is server-to-server traffic within the same part of the network. North-south traffic moves between access and core/Internet directions.

questions.question: Which term describes a LAN design with access and distribution layers only, where the core is collapsed into the distribution?
questions.explanation: Two-tier (collapsed core) is a LAN design with access and distribution layers only — the core has been collapsed into the distribution layer.

questions.question: What does HSRP provide?
options: First-hop redundancy via a virtual IP for default gateway redundancy
A tunneling protocol for multicast traffic
questions.explanation: HSRP (and VRRP) are first-hop redundancy protocols that provide a virtual IP for default gateway redundancy.
